name: Atong (India)

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Atong (India) (aton1241).

notes: Skeleton. Atong is a Bodo-Garo (Sino-Tibetan) language of Meghalaya, India, written in a Latin-based orthography. Base Latin consonants and vowels plus digraphs ch/sh/ng/ny and aspirates kh/ph/th are mapped. Atong is only marginally tonal; any tone, plus vowel-length distinctions, are left unencoded, raising the PER floor.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Atong (India) (aton1241)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
sources.notes: Classification reference for a skeleton-tier entry.
